description: Phase 29: Privacy-First Ad Targeting System - On-device interest model, contextual ads as default, behavioral opt-in, ad disclosure UI, strict CSP with no third-party tracking
steps: 1. Create `packages/privacy-ads/` package with package.json (deps: zod, @quant/common), tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts, and src/ directory. Register it in pnpm-workspace.yaml (already covered by wildcard). Create barrel export at src/index.ts.

2. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/types.ts` with all domain types: OnDeviceInterestModel (interests array, decay timestamps), CandidateAd (50 candidates per slot), RankedAd, AdSignal (contextual vs behavioral), AdDisclosure (signals used, explanation text), TargetingMode ('contextual' | 'behavioral'), PrivacyConfig, BrandSafetyConfig.

3. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/services/on-device-ranker.service.ts` - OnDeviceRankerService class: receives ~50 candidate ads per slot, ranks using local interest model, returns top 3. Interest model never leaves the device. Only aggregate feedback signals (clicked/dismissed boolean) are emitted. Include rankCandidates(), updateLocalInterests(), getDisclosure() methods.

4. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/services/contextual-targeting.service.ts` - ContextualTargetingService: default mode that targets ads by content the user is currently viewing only. Methods: extractContentSignals(pageContent), matchAdsByContext(signals, candidates), no user profile data needed.

5. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/services/behavioral-opt-in.service.ts` - BehavioralOptInService: manages user consent state (opt-in only, default false). Methods: getConsent(userId), setConsent(userId, boolean), isOptedIn(userId). When opted in, the on-device ranker can use behavioral signals.

6. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/services/ad-disclosure.service.ts` - AdDisclosureService: generates 'why this ad' explanation with exactly 1-2 signals per ad. Methods: generateDisclosure(ad, targetingMode, signals) -> AdDisclosure. Must always return non-empty disclosure for every ad served.

7. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/services/privacy-enforcer.service.ts` - PrivacyEnforcerService: validates that no third-party tracking requests are made. Methods: validateCSP(headers), auditAdRequest(request) -> no cross-site identifiers. Integrates with CSPGenerator from @quant/security to set strict CSP directives (no third-party cookies, no external tracking pixels).

8. Create `packages/privacy-ads/src/services/brand-safety.service.ts` - BrandSafetyService: content classification for advertiser safety. Methods: classifyContent(content) -> safety categories, isAdSafe(ad, content) -> boolean, getBlockedCategories(advertiserId).

9. Write comprehensive tests in `packages/privacy-ads/src/__tests__/` for each service: on-device-ranker.service.test.ts (ranks 50 candidates to 3, never exposes raw user features), contextual-targeting.service.test.ts (matches by page content only), behavioral-opt-in.service.test.ts (default off, consent gating), ad-disclosure.service.test.ts (always 1-2 signals per ad), privacy-enforcer.service.test.ts (blocks third-party tracking requests), brand-safety.service.test.ts.
10. Update barrel export `packages/privacy-ads/src/index.ts` to export all services, types, and schemas.
acceptance_criteria: packages/privacy-ads builds and typechecks without errors
All tests pass: on-device ranker selects top 3 from 50 candidates
Ad disclosure always returns 1-2 signals for every ad
Behavioral targeting is opt-in only (default off)
Privacy enforcer rejects requests with third-party tracking identifiers
No user profile data is ever included in ad request payloads sent to server
